title: Week 03 - Counting and Classification
description: Practice conditional counting, classification statistics, and remainder grouping.

questions.title: Count How Many Even Seat Numbers Are in a Class

description.background: The seats in a classroom are numbered from 1 to n. The teacher wants to pick out seats with even numbers to arrange for some students, so the number of even seat numbers needs to be counted.
description.task: Traverse all seat numbers from 1 to n, count the even seat numbers, and return the result.
description.output: Return the number of even seat numbers.
description.hint: A number divisible by 2 is even. Use a count variable to record how many seat numbers meet the condition.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count = 0
    for seat = 1, n do
        if seat % 2 == 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Count Multiples of 5 from 1 to n

description.background: The teacher is helping students practice number statistics. Given a positive integer n, students need to count from 1 to n, find all numbers divisible by 5, and count how many there are.
description.task: Loop from 1 to n, count all multiples of 5, and return the result.
description.output: Return the number of multiples of 5.
description.hint: Check each number from 1 to n. If a number has remainder 0 when divided by 5, add 1 to the count variable.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count = 0
    for number = 1, n do
        if number % 5 == 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Count How Many Odd Task Numbers There Are

description.background: A task list is numbered from 1 to n. According to the activity rule, odd-numbered tasks belong to group one and should be handled by students in that group. Count how many tasks group one must handle.
description.task: Traverse all task numbers from 1 to n, count the odd numbers, and return the result.
description.output: Return the count of odd-numbered tasks.
description.hint: If a number has remainder 1 after division by 2, it is odd. Use count to record the number of odd tasks.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count = 0
    for task = 1, n do
        if task % 2 == 1 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Count How Many Lucky Lottery Numbers There Are

description.background: A school lottery gives out lottery numbers from 1 to n. The activity rule says that only numbers divisible by 6 are lucky numbers. The staff need to count the lucky numbers in advance to prepare prizes.
description.task: Traverse all numbers from 1 to n, count the numbers divisible by 6, and return the result.
description.output: Return the number of lucky numbers.
description.hint: A lucky number must satisfy number % 6 == 0. Add 1 to the count variable whenever a number meets the condition.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count = 0
    for number = 1, n do
        if number % 6 == 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Count Regular Books by Library Number

description.background: Books in a library are numbered from 1 to n. The librarian defines books whose numbers are multiples of 5 as special books, and all other books as regular books. Count the regular books for classification.
description.task: Traverse all book numbers from 1 to n, count the numbers that are not multiples of 5, and return the number of regular books.
description.output: Return the number of values that are not multiples of 5.
description.hint: If a number has a nonzero remainder when divided by 5, it is not a special book and can be counted as a regular book.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count = 0
    for book = 1, n do
        if book % 5 ~= 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Count Athletes Whose Numbers Are Divisible by 3

description.background: After sports day registration, athletes are numbered from 1 to n. The teacher decides that students whose numbers are divisible by 3 will join the relay event, so the number of eligible athletes must be counted first.
description.task: Traverse all athlete numbers from 1 to n, count the numbers divisible by 3, and return the number of relay participants.
description.output: Return the count of numbers divisible by 3.
description.hint: Use count to record the number of eligible athletes. If a number has remainder 0 when divided by 3, add 1 to count.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local count = 0
    for athlete = 1, n do
        if athlete % 3 == 0 then
            count = count + 1
        end
    end
    return count
end

questions.title: Classify Library Books into Three Levels by Number

description.background: The library numbers books from 1 to n and classifies them into three groups by the remainder after division by 3. Remainder 0 goes into the first group, remainder 1 into the second group, and remainder 2 into the third group. The librarian needs to count how many books are in each group.
description.task: Traverse all book numbers from 1 to n, classify each number by its remainder after division by 3, and return the counts for the three groups.
description.output: Return three values: the counts for remainder 0, remainder 1, and remainder 2.
description.hint: Prepare three count variables: group0, group1, and group2. Use book % 3 to decide which count to increase.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local group0 = 0
    local group1 = 0
    local group2 = 0
    for book = 1, n do
        if book % 3 == 0 then
            group0 = group0 + 1
        elseif book % 3 == 1 then
            group1 = group1 + 1
        else
            group2 = group2 + 1
        end
    end
    return group0, group1, group2
end

questions.title: Count Game Tasks by Difficulty Number

description.background: A game system numbers tasks from 1 to n and assigns task difficulty cyclically by the remainder after division by 3. A remainder of 1 means easy, 2 means normal, and 0 means hard. Count how many tasks belong to each difficulty.
description.task: Traverse all task numbers from 1 to n, determine each task difficulty by its remainder, and count the easy, normal, and hard tasks separately.
description.output: Return three values: the counts of easy, normal, and hard tasks.
description.hint: Use three count variables: easy, normal, and hard. Increase the matching count according to task % 3.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local easy = 0
    local normal = 0
    local hard = 0
    for task = 1, n do
        if task % 3 == 1 then
            easy = easy + 1
        elseif task % 3 == 2 then
            normal = normal + 1
        else
            hard = hard + 1
        end
    end
    return easy, normal, hard
end

questions.title: Group Student Numbers by Remainder and Count Each Group

description.background: The teacher wants to split students numbered from 1 to n into three groups. Students whose number has remainder 0 after division by 3 enter group A, remainder 1 enter group B, and remainder 2 enter group C. Count how many students are in each group.
description.task: Traverse all student numbers from 1 to n, group each number by its remainder after division by 3, and return the counts for groups A, B, and C.
description.output: Return three values: the numbers of students in group A, group B, and group C.
description.hint: Prepare groupA, groupB, and groupC count variables. Use student % 3 to put each student into the matching group.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(n)
    local groupA = 0
    local groupB = 0
    local groupC = 0
    for student = 1, n do
        if student % 3 == 0 then
            groupA = groupA + 1
        elseif student % 3 == 1 then
            groupB = groupB + 1
        else
            groupC = groupC + 1
        end
    end
    return groupA, groupB, groupC
end
